notes: Encrypted automatic backups. When a backup passphrase is set, your automatic backups are now encrypted before they are written — so it is safe to keep them in a cloud folder (OneDrive, Google Drive); the file there is unreadable without the passphrase. Without a passphrase, backups are written in plain form as before (fine for a local, disk-encrypted drive). Restoring an encrypted backup on this computer is automatic; on a new computer, use 'Restore from encrypted backup' and type the passphrase. Existing unencrypted backups still restore normally.
